sourceLabel: City of St. John's 2026 Budget release (Council approval Dec 15, 2025)
hiddenReason: Provincial policing (RNC) means St. John's municipal budget has near-zero public-safety spend, which distorts the % comparison. Reactivate when the page methodology supports comparing cities with structurally different policing models.
contextNote: In Newfoundland and Labrador, policing is a provincial responsibility — the Royal Newfoundland Constabulary is funded by the province, not the municipality. St. John's also bundles water, sewer, and waste (25% of its budget) into general operating rather than running them as a separate utility. The result is a budget that's hard to compare cleanly as a % allocation.
dataStatusNote: Verified from the City's 2026 Budget release: total $366.0M, Water + Sewer + Waste 25% ($91.5M), Transportation $82M (incl. Metrobus & paratransit), Fire Department $29M. Important: in Newfoundland and Labrador, policing is a provincial responsibility — municipalities don't fund the Royal Newfoundland Constabulary, so the public-safety bucket reflects fire-only (very different from Moncton's $71M Protective Services). St. John's also bundles water/sewer/waste into general operating, unlike Moncton's separate utility — that's why the water-wastewater bucket is large here and zero for Moncton. Remaining buckets (parks/rec, planning, general government, debt, grants) are proportional estimates pending full transcription from the official 2026 Budget Book PDF.

nativeDepartments.name: St. John's Regional Fire Department (~$29M, confirmed)
Policing is a provincial responsibility in NL (Royal Newfoundland Constabulary) — not in the municipal budget
Transportation Services — roads, snow ($5.3M equipment replacement confirmed), traffic, sidewalks (confirmed $82M)
Metrobus / paratransit operating grant (+$1M increase confirmed)
Water, Sewer & Waste — bundled as the city's single largest investment (~25% of budget, confirmed)
Note: includes solid-waste collection in addition to water/sewer infrastructure
Corporate Services, Council, City Manager, Legal, IT, HR (estimated)
Fiscal Services — debt servicing, capital from operating, reserves; capital projects $22M confirmed (estimated total)
Grants (incl. St. John's Sports and Entertainment, community partnerships) (estimated)
